title: Starbucks vs Dunkin
shortAnswer: Starbucks dominates with premium positioning and global reach (40% US market share, 41k stores) while Dunkin' competes on value with $2 lower prices and franchise model.
prosA: Dominant global presence with 41,118 stores across 90 countries
Premium brand positioning allows higher margins and pricing power
Strong company-operated model provides better brand control
Leading market share of 40% in lucrative US coffee market
consA: Higher fixed costs from company-operated store model
Premium pricing limits customer base compared to value competitors
Higher operational complexity with direct store management
prosB: Franchise model enables faster expansion with lower capital risk
Value pricing attracts price-conscious consumers with $2 lower costs
Strong domestic performance with +10.4% two-year comp growth
Efficient operational model with lower overhead costs
consB: Smaller global footprint limits growth opportunities
Less brand control due to franchise-dominant model
Lower market share at 26% despite strong value proposition
verdict: Starbucks wins on scale and premium positioning with superior global reach and market share, while Dunkin' succeeds in the value segment with efficient franchise operations. Both models are working effectively in their respective market segments, with Starbucks dominating premium coffee experiences and Dunkin' capturing price-conscious consumers.
